diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden fall in urine output is a defining feature of oliguric AKI.
Identical symptom of 'not passing urine' — obstruction must be excluded by bladder scan.

symptoms.symptom_name: New breathlessness worse lying flat (orthopnoea proxy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute kidney injury with fluid overload
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluid the failing kidney cannot excrete produces pulmonary congestion and orthopnoea.
Orthopnoea is also a cardiac feature — overlap that requires in-person assessment to separate.
Altered mental status signals severe uraemia/hypoperfusion and marks an emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent severe gastroenteritis with poor fluid intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume depletion causing renal hypoperfusion is the initiating prerenal insult.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-dose NSAID (ibuprofen) plus ACE inhibitor (perindopril) use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: NSAID plus RAAS blockade on a hypovolaemic kidney removes both autoregulatory defences of glomerular filtration — a classic precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral peripheral pitting oedema to the calves

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute kidney injury with fluid overload
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sequestered fluid from impaired excretion, patient-observable over video.
Reflects ongoing volume depletion and hypotension perpetuating the insult.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension (BP 98/60) with tachycardia (HR 112) and orthostatic drop

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured hypotension/tachycardia confirming volume depletion — sealed; the AI infers hypoperfusion from lightheadedness, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bibasal fine inspiratory crepitations on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute kidney injury with early pulmonary oedema
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultatory sign of pulmonary congestion — requires a clinician's stethoscope, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty bladder on percussion / minimal residual on bedside scan (excludes retention)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-distended bladder excludes post-renal obstruction and confirms a renal cause — the AI cannot perform this and must keep retention on the differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid rise in serum creatinine / urea (reduced eGFR)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute creatinine rise defines and stages AKI — obtainable only on bloods, never via telehealth.
The immediately lethal complication; confirmable only on bloods/ECG — sealed, and the reason emergency escalation is mandatory.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dehydration + NSAID + ACE inhibitor followed by anuria is a textbook precipitant constellation that should trigger immediate recognition and drug cessation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute kidney injury with fluid overload, encephalopathy and hyperkalaemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fluid overload, encephalopathy and hyperkalaemia together mandate emergency-level escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent serum electrolytes, urea and creatinine (with potassium)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines and stages AKI and detects life-threatening hyperkalaemia — must be obtained emergently in person.
Detects hyperkalaemic changes that predict imminent arrhythmia.
Assesses metabolic acidosis and potassium rapidly.
Excludes post-renal obstruction as a reversible cause.
Assesses degree of fluid overload once the patient is in a monitored setting.
